task_completion.description: The session contains exactly the six required tracks and no extra unused tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour editor or mixer window, the track headers or mixer strips show exactly these six names: 'Kick Drum', 'Snare Drum', 'Bass DI', 'Electric Guitar', 'Lead Vocal', and 'Room Mic'. Default tracks like 'Audio 1' should not be visible.

task_completion.description: The panning for each track is set to the specified positions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the pan controls in the mixer or track headers. 'Electric Guitar' should be panned hard left, 'Snare Drum' slightly right, and all other tracks ('Kick Drum', 'Bass DI', 'Lead Vocal', 'Room Mic') should be centered.

task_completion.description: The gain (fader levels) for each track is set to the specified dB values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the faders or numerical gain readouts in the mixer. 'Kick Drum' and 'Snare Drum' should be around -3 dB, 'Bass DI' and 'Electric Guitar' around -6 dB, 'Lead Vocal' at 0 dB, and 'Room Mic' at -12 dB.

task_completion.description: The 'Room Mic' track is muted, and all other tracks are unmuted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'M' (Mute) button on the 'Room Mic' track is highlighted/active, while the 'M' buttons on all other tracks are inactive.

task_completion.description: The audio file is imported onto the 'Room Mic' track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region (waveform block) is visible on the timeline specifically within the 'Room Mic' track lane.

task_completion.description: Markers named 'Soundcheck' and 'Take 1' are placed on the timeline in the correct order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the markers/locations ruler at the top of the timeline, there are markers labeled 'Soundcheck' and 'Take 1', with 'Take 1' positioned to the right of 'Soundcheck'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Ardour GUI to configure the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application open with visible interactions (e.g., mixer open, track names changed, faders moved), rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent actually imported the audio file into the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waveform is visibly rendered in the track lane, indicating a real audio file was processed by Ardour, not just a text placeholder.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to directly modify the Ardour session XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) is open showing the `.ardour` session file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required tracks are 'Kick Drum', 'Snare Drum', 'Bass DI', 'Electric Guitar', 'Lead Vocal', and 'Room Mic'. Target panning: Snare ~60% Right, Guitar Hard Left (0%), all others Center (50%). Target gains: Kick/Snare -3.0 dB, Bass/Guitar -6.0 dB, Vocal 0.0 dB, Room Mic -12.0 dB (allow ±2.0 dB tolerance). The 'Room Mic' track must be muted, all others unmuted. An audio region must be present on the 'Room Mic' track. Markers 'Soundcheck' and 'Take 1' must be present on the timeline in that temporal order.
